name: restai
description: RESTai is an AIaaS (AI as a Service) open-source platform. Built on top of LlamaIndex & Langchain. Supports any public LLM supported by LlamaIndex and any local LLM suported by Ollama/vLLM/etc. Precise embeddings usage and tuning. Built-in image generation (Dall-E, SD, Flux) and dynamic loading generators.

commit.message: Add validation for user existence in get_current_username function
refactor: clean up whitespace and improve code readability in project routes
fix: update project options reference for embedding k value
fix: update project options reference for question model rerank settings
fix: update project options reference for question model k value
refactor: remove Pinecone and Redis vector store implementations and related configurations
refactor: remove unnecessary check for project type in project creation route
refactor: remove legacy embedding module and update embedding handling in brain and router files
fix: update embedding model attribute name for access validation
fix: correct embedding model attribute name in access validation
feat: add support for LangChain embeddings in get_embedding_class function
refactor: replace FunctionCallingAgent with FunctionAgent in agent.py
Implement code changes to enhance functionality and improve performance
refactor: update dependencies and implement MCP server with authentication middleware
refactor: implement API key encryption and decryption, and reorganize version utility
refactor: require authentication for transcript generation endpoint
refactor: streamline access token creation and enhance error handling in authentication
Removes 'diffusers' and 'accelerate' dependencies from project configuration and updates audio processing logic to handle mp3 files more efficiently.

Adds support for 'diffusers' and 'accelerate' libraries in project dependencies

Adds support for 'diffusers' and 'accelerate' libraries

Updates dependencies to include 'diffusers' and 'accelerate'
Enhances transcript generation logic with chunked outputs
Adds audio file conversion to mp3 using ffmpeg for compatibility
Improves error handling and temporary file management

Updates embedding class matching logic

Extends the case statement to support "Ollama" as an alias for
"OllamaEmbeddings", improving flexibility in embedding class
resolution.

No issue references provided.

Serializes LLM options before database insertion

Ensures `llmc.options` is serialized as JSON prior to saving in the database, improving compatibility and data consistency.

Refactors LLM options handling for type consistency

Updates LLMModel and related classes to use a dictionary type for options instead of a JSON string.

Adds field validators to handle serialization and deserialization of options, ensuring compatibility and reducing error-prone manual parsing.

Simplifies API key masking logic in LLM routers.

Improves code maintainability and prepares for future enhancements.

Removes redundant filter condition in project query

Simplifies query logic by removing a redundant filter condition
that checked for project creator ID, as it overlaps with existing
permissions logic.

Improves query readability and reduces unnecessary complexity.

Adds support for managing Ollama models via API

Introduces endpoints to retrieve and pull models from an Ollama instance.
Adds new Pydantic models for Ollama-related requests and responses.
Integrates error handling and dependency checks for the Ollama Python package.

Enhances API functionality for tool management.

Validates user team membership before adding to project

Ensures a user is only added to a project's user list if they belong to at least one of the teams associated with that project.

list users

Adds limited user response for non-admin queries

Implements logic to return a limited user model for regular users based on team membership.

Enhances user retrieval to restrict access to only team users while allowing admins to see all users.

Improves data handling and response structure for user listing.

Relates to user listing enhancement.

releases.name: v5.0.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Pinecone by @apocas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/74
* Organized environment variables by @nayan32biswas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/78
* fixes by @apocas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/79
* Semantic cache by @apocas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/80
* readme by @apocas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/81
* Update README.md by @eltociear in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/83
* Agents refactor by @apocas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/86
* typo by @apocas in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/87
* Update compose and Dockerfile by @hallowslab in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/85

## New Contributors
* @nayan32biswas made their first contribution in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/78
* @eltociear made their first contribution in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/83
* @hallowslab made their first contribution in https://github.com/apocas/restai/pull/85

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/apocas/restai/compare/v4.2.1...v5.0.0

releases.name: v4.2.0
releases.body:
**v4.2.0**
- Optional Colbert rerank
- Optional LLM rerank
- Streaming
- LLama-index major update
- Automatic Ollama pull
- LLMs dynamic management
- New frontend

readme_html:
Features

  • Projects: There are multiple project types, each with its own features. (rag, ragsql, inference, vision, router, agent)
  • Users: A user represents a user of the system. Each user may have access to multiple projects.
  • LLMs: Supports any public LLM supported by LlamaIndex. Which includes any local LLM supported by Ollama, LiteLLM, etc.
  • API: The API is a first-class citizen of RestAI. All endpoints are documented using Swagger.
  • Frontend: There is a frontend available at restai-frontend
  • Image Generation: Supports local and remote image generators. Local image generators are run in a separate process. New generators are easily added and loaded dynamically.
  • Proxy: Allows management of an OpenAI compatible proxy. LiteLLM is supported out of the box.

Project Types

RAG

  • Embeddings: You may use any embeddings model supported by llamaindex. Check embeddings definition.
  • Vectorstore: There are two vectorstores supported: ChromaDB and RedisVL
  • Retrieval: It features an embeddings search and score evaluator, which allows you to evaluate the quality of your embeddings and simulate the RAG process before the LLM. Reranking is also supported, ColBERT and LLM based.
  • Loaders: You may use any loader supported by llamaindex.
  • Sandboxed mode: RAG projects have "sandboxed" mode, which means that a locked default answer will be given when there aren't embeddings for the provided question. This is useful for chatbots, where you want to provide a default answer when the LLM doesn't know how to answer the question, reduncing hallucination.
  • Evaluation: You may evaluate your RAG agent using deepeval. Using the eval property in the RAG endpoint.

RAGSQL

  • Connection: Supply a MySQL or PostgreSQL connection string and it will automatically crawl the DB schema, using table and column names it’s able to figure out how to translate the question to sql and then write a response.

Agent

  • Zero-Shot ReAct Agents, specify which tools to use in the project and the agent will figure out how to use them to achieve the objective.

  • New tools are easily added. Just create a new tool in the tools folder and it will be automatically picked up by RESTai. Check the app/llms/tools folder for examples using the builtin tools.

  • Tools: Supply all the tools names you want the Agent to use in this project. (separated by commas)

  • Terminal: Core tool that allows the agent to execute commands via SSH. (using containerssh.io or similar is recommended)

Inference

Vision

  • img2text: RESTai supports virtually any vision model.

LLaVA

Image Generators

  • New generators are easily added. Just create a new tool in the generators folder and it will be automatically picked up by RESTai. Check the app/image/workers folder for examples using the builtin generators.
  • text2img: RESTai supports txt2image like Stable Diffusion, Flux, DallE, ...
  • img2img: RESTai supports img2img like BMBG2, ...

Flux1

Stable Diffusion

Router

  • Routes a message to the most suitable project. It's useful when you have multiple projects and you want to route the question to the most suitable one.
  • Routes: Very similar to Zero-Shot ReAct strategy, but each route is a project. The router will route the question to the project that has the highest score. It's useful when you have multiple projects and you want to route the question to the most suitable one.

LLMs

  • You may use any LLM provider supported by LlamaIndex.
  • Builtin LLMs supported:
    • Ollama
    • OllamaMultiModal
    • OpenAI
    • OpenAILike (anything that is OpenAI compatible)
    • Grok
    • Groq
    • Anthropic
    • LiteLLM
    • vLLM
    • Gemini
    • AzureOpenAI

Installation

  • RESTai uses uv to manage dependencies.

Architecture

Stateless

  • Ideal scenario for production environments. There is no state stored in the RESTai service.

Stateful

  • Ideal for small deployments, direct interaction with the GPU layer.

Development

  • make install
  • make dev (starts RESTai in development mode)

Production

  • make install
  • make start

Docker

  • Edit the .env file accordingly
  • docker compose --env-file .env up --build

You can specify profiles docker compose --profile redis --profile mysql .... to include additional components like the redis cache backend or a DB server, here are the supported profiles:

  • --profile redis Starts and sets redis as the cache backend
  • --profile mysql Starts and enables Mysql as the database server
  • --profile postgres Starts and enables Postgres as the database server

The variables MYSQL_HOST and POSTGRES_HOST should match the names of the respective services "mysql" and "postgres" and not localhost or 127.0.0.1 when using the containers

To delete everything or a specific container don't forget to pass the necessary profiles to the compose command, EX:

  • Removing everything docker compose --profile mysql --profile postgres down --rmi all
  • Removing singular database volume docker compose --profile mysql down --volumes

Note: the local_cache volume will also get removed since it's in the main service and not in any profile

API

  • Endpoints: All the API endpoints are documented and available at: Endpoints
  • Swagger: Swagger/OpenAPI documentation: Swagger

Frontend

  • Source code at https://github.com/apocas/restai-frontend.
  • make install automatically installs the frontend.

Tests

  • Tests are implemented using pytest. Run them with make test.

License
